frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: I mean yeah, she looks good, but sometimes it feels like too much effort yaar. Professional rehna alag hai, but girls who are actually confident don’t need so much makeup — natural is always better.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it evaluates a woman’s appearance in terms of how much makeup she should use and ties makeup use to insecurity or lack of confidence. It encodes the 'natural > artificial' preference and policing of female grooming as excessive effort.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A junior employee at a tech startup in Bengaluru. You are in the office cafeteria having lunch with three male colleagues after your female engineering manager just led a very strong all hands presentation. One colleague says she was impressive. You are responding and giving your actual opinion of her.
